instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-non-negative` that evaluates an expression once, signals an error if it is negative, and otherwise returns its value.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 SCORE))
            (WHEN (MINUSP #:G1) (ERROR "Expected non-negative, got ~A" #:G1))
            #:G1)
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 SCORE))
            (WHEN (MINUSP #:G112)
              (ERROR "Expected non-negative, got ~A" #:G112))
            #:G112)

--- stderr ---
